contentBlocks.original:
शान्ताकारं भुजगशयनं पद्मनाभं सुरेशं
विश्वाधारं गगनसदृशं मेघवर्णं शुभाङ्गम्
लक्ष्मीकान्तं कमलनयनं योगिभिर्ध्यानगम्यं
वन्दे विष्णुं भवभयहरं सर्वलोकैकनाथम्
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śāntākāraṃ bhujagaśayanaṃ padmanābhaṃ sureśaṃ
viśvādhāraṃ gaganasadṛśaṃ meghavarṇaṃ śubhāṅgam
lakṣmīkāntaṃ kamalanayanaṃ yogibhirdhyānagamyaṃ
vande viṣṇuṃ bhavabhayaharaṃ sarvalokaikanātham
contentBlocks.translation: Praising Lord Vishnu, who has a tranquil form, who rests on the serpent bed, whose navel bears a lotus (the source of creation), who is the lord of the Gods, the support of the universe, vast as the sky, with a dark hue like a raincloud and auspicious limbs; who is dear to Lakshmi, has lotus-like eyes, is accessible through the meditation of yogis, and is the remover of worldly fears, the one and only lord of all worlds.
contentBlocks.meanings: शान्ताकारं (śāntākāraṃ) – tranquil form (śānta:tranquil | ākāraṃ:form); भुजगशयनं (bhujagaśayanaṃ) – resting on serpent bed (bhujaga:serpent | śayanaṃ:bed); पद्मनाभं (padmanābhaṃ) – lotus-naveled (padma:lotus | nābhaṃ:navel); सुरेशं (sureśaṃ) – lord of the Gods (sura:God | īśaṃ:lord); विश्वाधारं (viśvādhāraṃ) – support of the universe (viśva:universe | ādhāraṃ:support); गगनसदृशं (gaganasadṛśaṃ) – vast as the sky (gagana:sky | sadṛśaṃ:like); मेघवर्णं (meghavarṇaṃ) – cloud-colored (megha:cloud | varṇaṃ:color); शुभाङ्गम् (śubhāṅgam) – auspicious limbs (śubha:auspicious | aṅgam:limbs); लक्ष्मीकान्तं (lakṣmīkāntaṃ) – dear to Lakshmi (lakṣmī:Lakshmi | kāntaṃ:dear); कमलनयनं (kamalanayanaṃ) – lotus-eyed (kamala:lotus | nayanaṃ:eye); योगिभिः (yogibhiḥ) – by yogis; ध्यानगम्यं (dhyānagamyaṃ) – accessible through meditation (dhyāna:meditation | gamyaṃ:accessible); वन्दे (vande) – I praise; विष्णुं (viṣṇuṃ) – Vishnu; भवभयहरं (bhavabhayaharaṃ) – remover of worldly fears (bhava:worldly | bhaya:fears | haraṃ:remover); सर्वलोकैकनाथम् (sarvalokaikanātham) – one lord of all worlds (sarva:all | loka:worlds | eka:one | nātham:lord);
